title: How the Industrial Revolution affected life in Britain

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Hi everyone, in this session I'll be presenting my research about the social history of Britain during the Industrial Revolution.
segments.zh: 大家好，本次讲座我将分享我关于工业革命时期英国社会史的研究。我主要考察了这段时期发生的种种变化如何影响了普通人的生活。这是一个新现象诞生的时代——即消费主义：买卖商品成为普通民众生活的重要组成部分。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I particularly looked at how ordinary lives were affected by changes that happened at that time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This was a time that saw the beginning of a new phenomenon: consumerism – where buying and selling goods became a major part of ordinary people's lives.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, it was in the 19th century that the quantity and quality of people's possessions was used as an indication of the wealth of the country.
segments.zh: 事实上，正是在19世纪，人们所拥有物品的数量与质量被用来衡量一个国家的财富。在此之前，绝大多数人几乎没有什么财产，而工业革命彻底改变了这一切。这段从18世纪中叶到19世纪末的时代里，商品制造方式的改进以及科技的进步引发了巨大的社会变革，几乎在各个关键领域都改变了所有人的生活。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Before this, the vast majority of people had very few possessions, but all that was changed by the Industrial Revolution.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This was the era from the mid-18th to the late 19th century, when improvements in how goods were made as well as in technology triggered massive social changes that transformed life for just about everybody in several key areas.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: First let's look at manufacturing.
segments.zh: 我们先来看制造业。谈到制造业，我们往往会联想到蒸汽机和煤炭这样的工业革命画面。的确，如果没有这些新的动力来源，工业革命根本不可能发生。它们标志着一个重要的转变——从此前长期占主导地位的水车和风车中转移出来。19世纪大部分时间里，最先进的产业是纺织业。这意味着时髦的布料、蕾丝和缎带都能被人人享用。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When it comes to manufacturing, we tend to think of the Industrial Revolution in images of steam engines and coal.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And it's true that the Industrial Revolution couldn't have taken place at all if it weren't for these new sources of power.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They marked an important shift away from the traditional watermills and windmills that had dominated before this.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The most advanced industry for much of the 19th century was textiles.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This meant that fashionable fabrics, and lace and ribbons were made available to everyone.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Before the Industrial Revolution, most people made goods to sell in small workshops, often in their own homes.
segments.zh: 工业革命之前，大多数人在小作坊里制作商品出售，通常就在自家。但如今出现了巨大的新机器，它们能够更快、更大规模地生产商品，因此需要更大的空间。于是大工厂建了起来，取代了小作坊，工人们不得不前往工厂上班。结果，大量人口从村庄迁往城镇。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But enormous new machines were now being created that could produce the goods faster and on a larger scale, and these required a lot more space.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So large factories were built, replacing the workshops, and forcing workers to travel to work.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, large numbers of people migrated from villages into towns as a result.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As well as manufacturing, there were new technologies in transport, contributing to the growth of consumerism.
segments.zh: 除了制造业以外，交通领域也出现了新技术，为消费主义的发展助力。18世纪运人载货很少、在坑洼道路上缓慢前行的马拉驿车和货车，逐渐被大量新修的运河所取代。这些运河尤其对货物运输至关重要。不过，运河很快又随着铁路的兴起而被淘汰——铁路成为在全国范围内运送货物和人员的主要方式。而这些交通工具运送的不仅仅是煤炭、铁、衣物等等——尤为重要的是，其中还包括报纸，这意味着成千上万的人不仅对国内正在发生的事情更加了解，还能读到商店里正在出售的商品信息。这也就鼓励他们去购买更多东西。因此，更快速的交通方式让分销效率大大提高：商品现在可以销往全国各地，而不仅仅局限于本地市场。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The horse-drawn stagecoaches and carts of the 18th century, which carried very few people and goods, and travelled slowly along poorly surfaced roads, were gradually replaced by the numerous canals that were constructed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These were particularly important for the transportation of goods.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The canals gradually fell out of use, though, as railways were developed, becoming the main way of moving goods and people from one end of the country to the other.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And the goods they moved weren't just coal, iron, clothes, and so on – significantly, they included newspapers, which meant that thousands of people were not only more knowledgeable about what was going on in the country, but could also read about what was available in the shops.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And that encouraged them to buy more.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So faster forms of transport resulted in distribution becoming far more efficient – goods could now be sold all over the country, instead of just in the local market.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The third main area that saw changes that contributed to consumerism was retailing.
segments.zh: 第三个推动消费主义变化的主要领域是零售业。商店的数量和质量迅速增长，尤其是小店铺受到重创，顾客纷纷涌向数量不断增长的百货公司——这是19世纪一种全新的零售形式。开这些百货商店的企业家想出了新办法来备货、吸引顾客：例如，改善店内照明，大幅提升商品的可见度。另一个让商品从外部看得更清楚的进步来自平板玻璃的使用，它让橱窗可以比以往大得多。此外还引入了新的商品促销方式。以前的重点在于向潜在顾客告知商品是否可得，而如今，广告业迎来爆炸式增长，一心想说服人们去购物。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The number and quality of shops grew rapidly, and in particular, small shops suffered as customers flocked to the growing number of department stores – a form of retailing that was new in the 19th century.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The entrepreneurs who opened these found new ways to stock them with goods, and to attract customers: for instance, improved lighting inside greatly increased the visibility of the goods for sale.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another development that made goods more visible from outside resulted from the use of plate glass, which made it possible for windows to be much larger than previously.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: New ways of promoting goods were introduced, too.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Previously, the focus had been on informing potential customers about the availability of goods; now there was an explosion in advertising trying to persuade people to go shopping.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Flanders claims that one of the great effects of the Industrial Revolution was that it created choice.
segments.zh: 弗兰德斯认为，工业革命最伟大的影响之一在于它创造了选择。许多先前只是奢侈品的东西——从糖到餐具——先是变成便利品，不久就变成必需品：没有糖或餐具的生活变得难以想象。这就跟我们如今离不开手机差不多！

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: All sorts of things that had previously been luxuries – from sugar to cutlery – became conveniences, and before long they'd turned into necessities: life without sugar or cutlery was unimaginable.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Rather like mobile phones these days!

questions.title: Questions 31–40
instructions: Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

items.prompt: For the first time, people's possessions were used to measure Britain's 31 ______.

pairs.q: people's possessions were used to measure
pairs.p: the quantity and quality of people's possessions was used as an indication
pairs.note: 'measure Britain's wealth' 对应 'used as an indication of the wealth of the country',用财富多寡衡量。

pairs.p: wealth of the country
pairs.note: Britain 即 the country;答案 wealth 直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 首次用人们的财产数量与质量来衡量国家的财富,答案填 wealth。

items.prompt: Developments in production of goods and in 32 ______ greatly changed lives.

pairs.p: improvements in how goods were made
pairs.note: 题干 developments in production 对应原文 improvements in how goods were made。

pairs.p: as well as in technology
pairs.note: in 后接的正是 technology,与题干 'in 32' 对应。

paraphrase.explain: 商品生产的改进 + 科技的改进共同引发了巨变,答案填 technology。

items.prompt: The Industrial Revolution would not have happened without the new types of 33 ______ that were used then.

pairs.q: would not have happened without the new types of
pairs.p: couldn't have taken place at all if it weren't for these new sources of
pairs.note: would not have happened without = couldn't have taken place if it weren't for,虚拟条件同义替换。

pairs.p: power
pairs.note: 答案词 power 直接出现在 new sources of power。

paraphrase.explain: 如果没有新的动力(power)来源,工业革命根本不可能发生,答案填 power。

items.prompt: The leading industry was 34 ______ (its products became widely available).

pairs.p: The most advanced industry for much of the 19th century was
pairs.note: leading = most advanced,都表示'最先进/最领先的'。

pairs.p: fashionable fabrics, and lace and ribbons were made available to everyone
pairs.note: 'made available to everyone' = became widely available。

paraphrase.explain: 19世纪最主要的产业是纺织业(textiles),答案填 textile(s)。

items.prompt: New 35 ______ made factories necessary and so more people moved into towns.

pairs.p: large factories were built
pairs.note: 'made factories necessary' 与 'large factories were built' 对应,都指工厂的出现。

pairs.p: enormous new machines
pairs.note: New + 名词 对应 enormous new machines,机器是关键的'新事物'。

pairs.p: large numbers of people migrated from villages into towns
pairs.note: moved into towns = migrated into towns。

paraphrase.explain: 新出现的大机器(machines)需要更大空间,导致工厂建立、人口涌入城镇,答案填 machines。

items.prompt: – greater access to 36 ______ made people more aware of what they could buy in shops.

pairs.p: the goods they moved weren't just coal, iron, clothes
pairs.note: 交通改善使人们更容易接触/获得报纸等物,即 greater access。

pairs.q: made people more aware of what they could buy in shops
pairs.p: could also read about what was available in the shops
pairs.note: 读到商店里在卖什么 = 更了解能买到什么。

pairs.p: newspapers
pairs.note: 答案 newspapers 直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 报纸(newspapers)通过新交通被广泛运送,让人们更了解商店里的商品,答案填 newspapers。

items.prompt: – when shopping, people were not limited to buying 37 ______ goods.

pairs.p: goods could now be sold all over the country
pairs.note: 题干 'when shopping, people were not limited to buying 37 goods' 与原文 'goods could now be sold all over the country, instead of just in the local market' 对应。

pairs.p: instead of just in the local market
pairs.note: 不再局限于购买本地商品 = 不再只在本地市场买卖。

pairs.p: local
pairs.note: 答案 local 直接出现在 'the local market' 中。

paraphrase.explain: 购物时不再局限于购买本地(local)商品,答案填 local。

pairs.p: improved lighting inside greatly increased the visibility of the goods for sale
pairs.note: 'better 38' 与 'improved lighting inside' 对应,better = improved。

pairs.p: lighting
pairs.note: 答案 lighting 直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 店内更好的照明(lighting)让商品可见度提升,答案填 lighting。

pairs.p: made goods more visible from outside
pairs.note: 从店外能看到商品 = 商品从外面更可见。

pairs.p: windows to be much larger than previously
pairs.note: bigger = much larger,答案 windows 直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 使用平板玻璃后橱窗(windows)可以做得比以前大得多,答案填 windows。

items.prompt: • 40 ______ that was persuasive became much more common.

pairs.p: advertising trying to persuade people to go shopping
pairs.note: 'persuasive' 对应 'trying to persuade people',答案 advertising 直接出现。

pairs.p: there was an explosion in advertising
pairs.note: 'an explosion in advertising' = became much more common,广告激增。

paraphrase.explain: 以说服为目的的广告(Advertising)变得非常普遍,答案填 Advertising。
